---
title: "Muscle_Splicing_P1B"
author: "Christopher Jin"
date: '2025-07-08'
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(MotrpacRatTraining6mo)
library(MotrpacRatTraining6moData)
library(SGSeq)
library(TxDb.Rnorvegicus.UCSC.rn6.refGene)
library(dplyr)
library(Rsamtools)
library(limma)
library(parallel)
library(BiocParallel)
library(here)
library(biomaRt)
library(TMSig)
library(MotrpacHumanPreSuspensionData)
library(conflicted)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(edgeR)


source(file.path(here(), "data-raw", "raw_omics_data", "splicing_helper_functions.R"))
gtf_file_path = file.path(here(), "data", "Figures", "Splicing", "Rattus_norvegicus.mRatBN7.2.108.gtf")
num_cores = 8

conflicts_prefer(dplyr::select,
                 dplyr::filter,
                 dplyr::rename)

desired_tissues = c("gastroc", "vastus")
#, #exclude ovary for now--that will go into a different report. 
# "ovaries" = "Ovaries Powder"
# plot_rn7_data(assay = "TRNSCRPT",
#               tissue = "HEART",
#               feature = "NEB")
```

# Introduction:

I'm detailing the rationale, and methodology above, in these sections.
The implementation details can be found below.

![SGSeq implementation image](images_to_include/SGSeq.png)

I'm using the tool SGSeq to calculate and measure splicing events
(<https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0156132>), which was recommended
from archana and has previously been used in a cardiac-specific rat
paper. Here is a helpful overview of the general thought process of the analysis 
steps.

## General logistics & tools

The original bam files, mapped to rn7, are available:
`gs://motrpac-data-raw-pass1b-06/pass1b-06/transcriptomics/*` relevant
bam files have all been downloaded to one combined folder in
`data/bams/`

the GTF file file is located
`gs://rna-seq_araja/references/rn/mRatBN7.2/v108/Rattus_norvegicus.mRatBN7.2.108.gtf`

The Gene mapping is located
`gs://motrpac-data-hub/pass1b-06/analysis/resources/motrpac-mappings-master_feature_to_gene.txt`
See the `make_gene_mapping` function for how this is actually used.

The RNAseq metadata and general experimental design information is 
from `MotrpacRatTraining6moData`. 


## Analysis Overview and Parameters:

### Filtering:

Each transcript must have an average percent spliced in of
10%.

We require individual transcripts to have a minimum number of average
counts of #.

After those two above filtering steps, we remove any cases with one single 
transcript::gene mapping (if there is only a single transcript, no other spliced
form is eligible for comparison).



So in this section I've basically just followed the default parameters except
I've made a 0.1 Splicing frequency to focus on larger effect sizes.
```{r set up SGSeq df, eval = FALSE}
local_bams = file.path(here(), "data", "bam")

tissue_mapping = list(
  "gastroc" = "Gastrocnemius Powder",
  "vastus" = "Vastus Lateralis Powder"
)

#-----these lines are agnostic to tissue-----
anno = SGSeq::importTranscripts(gtf_file_path)
target_chr = paste0("chr", c(1:20,"X", "Y"))
txdb = GenomeInfoDb::keepSeqlevels(anno, target_chr, pruning.mode="coarse")
GenomeInfoDb::seqlevelsStyle(txdb) = "UCSC"
txf_ucsc = convertToTxFeatures(txdb)
#--------------------------------

#-------for each tissue we just follow instructions--------
# The parse_filter_counts is the only step that's outside of instructions
# But that function just applies all the filtering steps as described at the top
for(tissue in desired_tissues){
  SGSeq_Meta = set_up_SGSeq_meta(tissue_mapping[[tissue]])
  sgfc_ucsc = analyzeFeatures(SGSeq_Meta, 
                              features = txf_ucsc, 
                              cores = num_cores, 
                              psi = .1) #PSI 0 is default
  #its splice frequency, so .1 = 10%, i think
  saveRDS(sgfc_ucsc, file = file.path(here(), "data", tissue, "sgfc_10psi.RDS"))
  
  sgvc_pred = analyzeVariants(sgfc_ucsc, cores = num_cores)
  sgv = rowRanges(sgvc_pred)
  sgvc = getSGVariantCounts(sgv, sample_info = SGSeq_Meta, cores = num_cores)
  saveRDS(sgvc, file = file.path(here(), "data", tissue, "sgvc_10psi.RDS"))
  
  #this parsing and filtering step is HIGHLY important
  #I'd highly recommend reading the documentation in `parse_filter_counts`
  #to describe whats going on 
  parsed_counts = parse_filter_counts(sgvc, tissue)
  saveRDS(parsed_counts, file = file.path(here(), "data", tissue, "counts_meta.RDS"))
}
```

So now that we have datasets available, and properly processed through the
filtering criteria, lets go ahead and run models.

## Rationale for Likelihood ratio tests
This model essentially tests if information about any level of group and timepoint
helps to explain the variance found in every given feature at all. It could mean
that one group and timepoint fits results well, it could mean all of group and timepoint
helps to improve the model fit. 

```{r, eval = F}
for(tissue in desired_tissues){
  tissue_parsed_filt_counts = load_parsed_counts(selected_tissue = tissue)
  sample_metadata = tissue_parsed_filt_counts[["sample_metadata"]]
  qc_norm = t(tissue_parsed_filt_counts[["qc_norm"]]) 
  features_to_test = colnames(qc_norm) = qc_norm[rownames(qc_norm) == "variantName",]
  
  gene_mapping = tissue_parsed_filt_counts[["qc_norm"]] %>% 
    dplyr::select(geneName, variantName)
  
  #here we combine the dataset with the metadata to make sure that we can describe 
  #the actual rats and the groups/sex that they correspond to. 
  qc_meta = qc_norm %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    filter(rownames(.) != "geneName" & rownames(.) != "variantName") %>%
    #because geneName is a list it gets all weird, so i have to reconv back to num.
    mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric)) %>%
    mutate(viallabel = rownames(.)) %>%
    left_join(., sample_metadata, by = "viallabel") %>%
    mutate(group_timepoint = interaction(sex, group))
  
  #so here is the LRT to find 
  lrt_results = lapply(features_to_test, function(feature) {
    full_formula = as.formula(paste(feature, "~ group_timepoint"))
    null_formula = as.formula(paste(feature, "~ 1"))
    
    full_model = lm(full_formula, data = qc_meta)
    null_model = lm(null_formula, data = qc_meta)
    
    test = anova(null_model, full_model)
    
    tibble(
      variantName = feature,
      p_value = test$`Pr(>F)`[2],
      F_statistic = test$F[2],
      df1 = test$Df[2],
      df2 = test$Res.Df[2],
      var_explained = summary(full_model)$r.squared
    )
  }) %>% 
    bind_rows() %>%
    left_join(., gene_mapping, by = "variantName") %>%
    fit_gene_mapping() %>%
    mutate(tissue = tissue) %>%
    filter(!chromosome_name %in% c("X", "Y")) %>%
    mutate(adj_p_value = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH")) %>%
    arrange(adj_p_value) 
  
  # saveRDS(lrt_results, file = file.path(here(), "data", tissue, "LRT_Results.RDS"))
}
```

Printout of LRT with info about splicing event. 
```{r, eval = F}
for(tissue in desired_tissues){
  lrt_results = readRDS(file.path(here(), "data", tissue, "LRT_Results.RDS")) 
  
  tissue_parsed_filt_counts = load_parsed_counts(selected_tissue = tissue)
  tissue_anno = tissue_parsed_filt_counts$counts_annotated %>%
    select(from, to, type, variantName)
  
  print_out = lrt_results %>%
    select(variantName, gene_symbol, tissue, entrez_gene, F_statistic, adj_p_value, chromosome_name) %>%
    left_join(., tissue_anno, by = "variantName")
  write.csv(print_out,
            file = file.path(here(), "data", tissue, paste0(tissue, "_LRT_Results.csv")),
            row.names = FALSE)
  
}
```


## Enrichment Analysis
Now lets take a quick detour to visualize the results for the LRT. 
To keep things consistent with the the methodologies used by Tyler for all of the other figures,
we use the same set filtering methods that he implements. See the Splicing results
in the other notebook to generate the enrichments. 

```{r}
for(tissue in desired_tissues){
  if(tissue == "vastus") enrichment_code = "VL"
  if(tissue == "gastroc") enrichment_code = "GN"
  
  enrichment_res = read.csv(
    file.path(here(),
              "Figures",
              "Splicing",
              "data",
              sprintf("%s_CAMERA.csv", enrichment_code))
  ) %>%
    rename(GeneSet = Set,
           NGenes = NGenesRat)
  
  plot_for_camera = plot_enrichment_bubble(enrichment_res,
                                           custom_title = paste(tissue, "LRT Enrichments"))
  print(plot_for_camera)
}
```
## Junction plotting

Now we use the SGSeq built in functions to visualize some of the actual changes.

Notes:

So these visualize all of the splicing events for a whole gene, instead of just the actual
event that is significant, and in most cases the events are fully consistent across
some other junctions. 

I think in general these plots are brought up as one of the default plotting functions
for the SGSeq package, but I don't find them super useful. Maybe skip these plots. 

```{r, eval = TRUE}
for(tissue in desired_tissues){
  #-----so we load this stuff just to make sure the plots we make are actually
  #------------in the right order for the heatmaps--------
  parsed_counts = load_parsed_counts(selected_tissue = tissue)
  sample_metadata = parsed_counts$sample_metadata %>%
    arrange(group, sex) %>%
    pull(viallabel)
  #-------------------------------------------------------------
  
  #These are just the DA
  selected_differential_results = load_differential_splicing(selected_tissue = tissue) %>%
    filter(adj_p_value < 0.05) %>%
    pull(variantName)
  
  #------------these are the results that actually annotate junction events 
  #and we just pull out the info for the genes and variants that have 
  sgfc_ucsc = load_sgfc(selected_tissue = tissue)
  sgvc = load_sgvc(selected_tissue = tissue) 
  #so these are the mapping events that we're actually interested in
  feature_metadata_mapped = elementMetadata(sgvc) %>% 
    as.data.frame() %>%
    mutate(variantName = gsub("[/,]", "_", variantName)) %>%
    filter(variantName %in% selected_differential_results) %>%
    head(5) %>%
    pull(geneID)
  #--------------------------------------------------------
  
  #and now we just plot all the junction events and stuff
  #for some reason rowv needs to be named
  sample_order = match(sgfc_ucsc$sample_name, sample_metadata)
  names(sample_order) = sgfc_ucsc$sample_name
  lapply(feature_metadata_mapped, function(single_geneID){
    plotFeatures(sgfc_ucsc, 
                 geneID = single_geneID, 
                 Rowv = NULL,
                 include = "junctions", 
                 main = single_geneID)
  })
  
}
```



## Single isoform bargraphs

So here, for each actual variant considered to be differentially spliced, we
make a simple representation of what % of all variants this single
variant represents. Essentially, out of all variants we can measure for a given 
gene, this isoform variant appears ~n% of the time for this sample. 

In a lot of cases, although considered statistically significant, the absolute
magnitude of changes is relatively small. 
```{r, eval = TRUE}
for(tissue in desired_tissues){
  #------here we want to compare to original DA results, so we use the motrpac ID for 1B data
  if(tissue == "vastus") motrpac_code = "SKM-VL"
  if(tissue == "gastroc") motrpac_code = "SKM-GN"
  #---------------------------------
  norm_counts = load_parsed_counts(selected_tissue = tissue)
  sig_lrt_results = load_differential_splicing(selected_tissue = tissue) %>%
    filter(adj_p_value < 0.05) %>%
    head(5)  %>%
    pull(variantName)
  
  #--------this is kinda copied from parse filter counts------ 
  #for the conversion to %
  percent_counts = norm_counts$counts_annotated %>%
    filter(mean_counts > 5) %>% #arbitrary but less than 5 counts seems very low
    group_by(geneName) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ . / sum(.) * 100)) %>% #this is percentage
    #if there are no counts entirely for the whole gene for some columns, we just force it to 0%.
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ replace(., . == "NaN", 0)))
  percent_counts = fit_gene_mapping(percent_counts)
  
  
  for(single_feature in sig_lrt_results){
    relevant_counts = percent_counts %>%
      filter(variantName == single_feature)  %>%
      t() %>%
      as.data.frame() 
    #----these are just for annotation and adding some more info to the plots
    colnames(relevant_counts) = relevant_counts[rownames(relevant_counts) == "gene_symbol",]
    variant_type = relevant_counts[rownames(relevant_counts) == "variantType",]
    #-------------------------------------------------
    
    #------now we pull the 1B dataset-------
    ensembl_gene_name = percent_counts %>% 
      filter(variantName == single_feature) %>%
      pull(ensembl_gene)
    norm_plot_1B = plot_rn7_data(feature_ID = ensembl_gene_name,
                                 scale_x_by_time = FALSE,
                                 add_gene_symbol = TRUE)
    #------
    relevant_counts = relevant_counts %>%
      mutate(viallabel = rownames(.)) %>%
      right_join(., norm_counts$sample_metadata, by = "viallabel") 
    relevant_counts[,1] = as.numeric(relevant_counts[,1])
    gene_name = colnames(relevant_counts)[1]
    
    p_value = load_differential_splicing(selected_tissue = tissue) %>%
      filter(variantName == single_feature) %>%
      pull(adj_p_value) %>%
      signif(3)

    pdf(file = file.path(here(), "plots", "trajectory_splicing", paste0(tissue, gene_name, ".pdf")),
        width = 5,
        height = 5)
    plot = ggplot(relevant_counts, aes(x = factor(group, levels = c("control", "1w", "2w", "4w", "8w")), 
                                       y = .data[[gene_name]])) + 
      stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 5, ) +
      stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 5, shape = 1, stroke = 1) +
      stat_summary(fun.data = mean_cl_normal, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom",
            axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + 
      xlab(paste(tissue, "Isoform %", "LRT Adj. P-Value: ", p_value)) + 
      ylab(paste(gene_name, "variant type:", variant_type))
    gridExtra::grid.arrange(norm_plot_1B, plot, ncol = 1)
    dev.off()
  }
}

```


```{r}
gastroc_da = load_differential_splicing(selected_tissue = "gastroc")
vastus_da = load_differential_splicing(selected_tissue = "vastus")

lrt_sig = rbind(gastroc_da, vastus_da) %>% filter(adj_p_value < 0.05)
lrt_sig
```

Checking if any of our interesting features related splicing are also
differentially regulated at an overall transcript level. (None are)

The DA is from pass 1B rn6, but Pauline reran the rn7 analysis and there 
are also no significant features within our interesting splicing dataset. 


```{r}
TRNSCRPT_SPLICING_VL_CAMERA = read.csv(
  file.path(here(),
            "Figures",
            "Splicing",
            "data",
            "VL_CAMERA.csv")
) %>%
  mutate(Contrast = "Vastus")

TRNSCRPT_SPLICING_GN_CAMERA = read.csv(
  file.path(here(),
            "Figures",
            "Splicing",
            "data",
            "GN_CAMERA.csv")
) %>%
  mutate(Contrast = "Gastroc")

Splicing_muscles = rbind(TRNSCRPT_SPLICING_VL_CAMERA, 
                         TRNSCRPT_SPLICING_GN_CAMERA)

# Select the top 6 terms by cluster
top_terms <- Splicing_muscles %>% 
  filter(PAdj < 0.05) %>% 
  group_by(Contrast) %>%
  slice_min(PValue, n = 10L) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  pull(Set) %>% 
  unique()

Splicing_muscles <- filter(Splicing_muscles, Set %in% top_terms) %>% 
  mutate(logP = -log10(PValue))

# Dynamic heatmap height. Each cell of the heatmap is 14 pt, by default
height <- convertUnit(length(top_terms) * unit(20, "pt"), "in")
height <- max(as.numeric(height) + 0.2, 4)

# Dynamic heatmap width
row_label_width <- ComplexHeatmap::max_text_width(Splicing_muscles[["Set"]])
row_label_width <- convertUnit(row_label_width, "in")

width <- convertUnit(15 * unit(15, "pt"), "in")
width <- as.numeric(width + row_label_width) + 2.5

enrichmap(x = Splicing_muscles,
          plot_sig_only = FALSE,
          n_top = Inf, # plot all terms
          statistic_column = "logP",
          set_column = "Set",
          padj_column = "PAdj",
          padj_legend_title = "BH Adjusted\nP-Value",
          contrast_column = "Contrast",
          heatmap_args = list(
            cluster_rows = FALSE,
            rect_gp = gpar(fill = "white", col = "grey85"),
            heatmap_legend_param = list(
              title = latex2exp::TeX(
                "$\\bf{$-$log_{10}(P$-$Value)}$")
            ),
            clustering_distance_rows = "pearson",
            column_names_side = "top",
            # column_names_rot = 0,
            # column_names_centered = TRUE,
            column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = unit(16, "pt"),#unit(9, "pt"),
                                   fontface = "bold")
          ),
          color = c("white", "#503080"),
          filename = file.path("plots", "Splicing_CAMERA.pdf"),
          height = height, width = width)
```
